title: Piquant Dense Thai Pork Moo Ping
description: A herbal and dense Thai street food dish featuring kaffir lime leaf, this recipe is a low calorie friendly version of the classic moo ping, with a katsuramuki cut for a beautiful presentation.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, garlic, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the katsuramuki cut pork shoulder to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium high heat.
Remove the pork from the marinade and cook for 5 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Transfer the pork to a cutting board and let rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the pork into thin strips and serve with a sprinkle of salt and a drizzle of coconut milk.
Garnish with fresh kaffir lime leaves and serve immediately.
Heat the vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium heat and fry the kaffir lime leaves until crispy.
Drain the fried kaffir lime leaves on paper towels and use as garnish.
